{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/151374244?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Wernerina/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Wernerina/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Wernerina/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Wernerina/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/Wernerina", "id": 151374244, "login": "Wernerina", "node_id": "U_kgDOCQXJpA", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Wernerina/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Wernerina/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Wernerina/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Wernerina/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/Wernerina/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/Wernerina", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-35171", "cvss": {"score": 9.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 9.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-94", "name": "Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-502", "name": "Deserialization of Untrusted Data"}], "description": "### Impact

This is a **critical remote code execution (RCE)** vulnerability caused by unsafe use of `logging.config.dictConfig()` with user-controlled input.

Kedro allows the logging configuration file path to be set via the `KEDRO_LOGGING_CONFIG` environment variable and loads it without validation. The logging configuration schema supports the special `()` key, which enables arbitrary callable instantiation. An attacker can exploit this to execute arbitrary system commands during application startup.

---

### Patches

The vulnerability is fixed by introducing validation that rejects the unsafe `()` factory key in logging configurations before passing them to `dictConfig()`.

#### Fixed in
- Kedro 1.3.0

Users should upgrade to this version as soon as possible.

---

### Workarounds

If upgrading is not immediately possible:

- Do not allow untrusted input to control the `KEDRO_LOGGING_CONFIG` environment variable  
- Restrict write access to logging configuration files  
- Avoid using externally supplied or dynamically generated logging configs  
- Manually validate logging YAML to ensure it does not contain the `()` key  

These mitigations reduce risk but do not fully eliminate it.

---

### References

- Python logging configuration documentation: https://docs.python.org/3/library/logging.config.html#logging-config-dictschema  
- CWE-94: Code Injection — https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/94.html", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00714, "percentile": 0.49501}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-9cqf-439c-j96r", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-03T03:48:48Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-9cqf-439c-j96r", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-9cqf-439c-j96r"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-35171"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-04-06T18:16:43Z", "published_at": "2026-04-03T03:48:48Z", "references": ["https://github.com/kedro-org/kedro/security/advisories/GHSA-9cqf-439c-j96r", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-35171", "https://github.com/pypa/advisory-database/tree/main/vulns/kedro/PYSEC-2026-72.yaml", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-9cqf-439c-j96r"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/kedro-org/kedro/security-advisories/GHSA-9cqf-439c-j96r", "severity": "critical", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/kedro-org/kedro", "summary": "Kedro has Arbitrary Code Execution via Malicious Logging Configuration", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-06T00:23:46Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-9cqf-439c-j96r", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "1.3.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "kedro"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 1.3.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Kedro has Arbitrary Code Execution via Malicious Logging Configuration
